summary: The implementation matches the oauth-consent-and-token-runtime spec for this task. /oauth/token dispatches client_credentials, validates enabled client credentials, issues client-subject RS256 JWTs with grant_type=client_credentials, uses client credentials TTL, persists machine tokens with nullable oauthUserId, and computes endpoint permissions as all allowed endpoints or the requested endpoint-scope intersection. Unit and E2E coverage exercise valid issuance, scope narrowing, no-scope full allowed permissions, invalid credentials, and disabled clients. The mandatory deterministic checks are reported passing.
satisfied_exit_criteria: Client credentials tokens can be issued for valid clients.
Scope intersection behavior is tested in unit and @oauth-client-credentials E2E coverage.
Invalid, missing, wrong-secret, and disabled clients fail with invalid_client/401 behavior.
Token claims distinguish client_credentials with sub=client:<client_id> from authorization_code user-subject tokens.
Required commands passed: npm run test:unit, npm run test:e2e -- --grep @oauth-client-credentials, and npm run verify.
Task maps directly to docs/product-specs/oauth-consent-and-token-runtime.md and does not implement out-of-scope discovery, bearer enforcement, token UI/revocation, or refresh tokens.
